item.title: Growth Intelligence Brief #20
item.description: Is Reddit done? Is Yellow Pages back? Why did Yelp hit a new record this week?

item.title: Make your prompt tracking more accurate this week
item.description: AI Visibility is measurable. The mistake is running it like a rank tracker.

item.title: What to do now that AIOs turned search into reading sessions
item.description: Intent compression breaks the segmentation SEO ran on for 20 years. Your content strategy map survives, and your per-page optimization might not.

item.title: Growth Intelligence Brief #19
item.description: An updated version of the brief, the SaaS slide and Google's agentic SEO press release.

item.title: Users behave differently in AI Overviews vs. AI Mode
item.description: A new clickstream study of 846K sessions shows AI Overviews turn the SERP into a comparison environment, while AI Mode produces acceptance

item.title: Reasoning lift: What happens to AI visibility when AI thinks harder
item.description: High reasoning fires 4.6x more searches cites a nearly different web.

item.title: Growth Intelligence Brief #18
item.description: Snapchat and Nextdoor grow at rapid pace, Amazon builds LLM knowledge graph and Microsoft is leading the game in AEO transparency.

item.title: The Consensus Gap
item.description: 91% of AI citations show up in only 1 engine

item.title: AI changed my work. And yours, too.
item.description: When the cost of building anything collapses to near zero, what's actually scarce? What does that mean for how we work?

item.title: The AI skills salary premium
item.description: The SEO job market quietly repriced around AI, but 4 out of 5 of the new higher-paying roles don't say AI in the title.

item.title: The ghost citation problem
item.description: New data from 3,981 domains across 115 prompts, 14 countries, and 4 AI search engines

item.title: Growth Intelligence Brief #17
item.description: Welcome to another Growth Intelligence Brief, where organic growth leaders discover what matters - getting insights into the bigger picture and guidance on how to stay ahead of the competition.

item.title: Shorter, Focused Content Wins in ChatGPT
item.description: We analyzed 815,000 query-page pairs. The "ultimate guide" strategy produces worse citation results than a focused shorter page.

item.title: How consumers navigate high-stakes purchases in AI Mode
item.description: A new user behavior study showing how AI Mode users accept shortlists built by the LLM, while classic Google search users build them themselves.

item.title: Growth Intelligence Brief #16
item.description: Welcome to another Growth Intelligence Brief, where organic growth leaders discover what matters - getting insights into the bigger picture and guidance on how to stay ahead of the competition.

item.title: The science of what AI actually rewards
item.description: Most AI SEO writing advice doesn’t hold at scale. This memo breaks down what AI actually rewards inside the content it reads and across verticals.

item.title: The science of how AI picks its sources
item.description: I analyzed over 21K citations to understand the impact of content length, depth and focus.

item.title: The brand tax: How Google profits from demand you already own
item.description: Most brands' highest-ROAS campaigns are capturing demand that already exists. Contentsquare's 99B-session benchmark shows the math is getting worse.

item.title: Growth Intelligence Brief #15
item.description: GPTBot hit a zero-authority site 470x harder than Googlebot. New data shows that doesn't translate to citations. This week: the 2 filters that actually determine AI visibility.

item.title: Organic rankings vs. product grids: The new e-commerce divide
item.description: I analyzed 4K+ keywords and 40K product grids: The gap between classic SEO winners and product grid winners is enormous.
